name: Armstrong Co. Federal Emp.
description_short: Allegheny Central Employees Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving Fayette County, PA with loans, certificates, and online banking since 1935.
description_long:
Allegheny Central Employees Federal Credit Union (ACE FCU) was originally chartered in 1935 as the West Penn Connellsville District Employees Federal Credit Union to serve railroad and company employees. The institution has evolved significantly, expanding from serving specific employer groups to becoming a community-chartered credit union in recent years. Today, it serves anyone who lives, works, or worships in Fayette County, Pennsylvania, operating as a full-service financial institution from its headquarters in Dunbar, PA.

ACE FCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including share savings accounts, share certificates (currently offering 3.50% APR on 9-month specials), personal loans, auto loans (new and used), home equity lines of credit, and Visa credit cards. Members can access online banking, apply for loans digitally, and utilize skip-a-pay options. The credit union maintains a modest but experienced staff of three and is governed by a seven-member Board of Directors. They provide standard NCUA insurance protection on member deposits and maintain competitive rates, recently lowering home equity rates by 0.75% across multiple terms (5.49% for 60 months, 5.74% for 84 months, 5.99% for 120 months).

ACE FCU distinguishes itself through member-focused perks including zero transfer fees on Visa credit cards (compared to competitor rates of 3-5%), birthday month loan discounts of 0.50%, and affordable skip-a-pay options at $30 per loan available twice yearly. The institution emphasizes personal service, inviting members to visit their office and meet staff. Their online loan applications are streamlined and allow document uploads for faster processing. The credit union actively engages members through annual dinner meetings and maintains transparent communication about rate changes and service updates.

ACE FCU is best suited for individuals who already work, live, or worship in Fayette County and value personalized service from a small, stable institution. As a credit union with only three staff members and limited physical presence, they may have slower processing times compared to larger institutions, and their product offerings are more basic than regional or national competitors. The organization appears well-managed and financially sound, but their size limits convenience factors like extensive branch networks or 24/7 support.
services: Visa Platinum credit cards with zero transfer fees
Online loan applications with document upload
pros: Zero transfer fees on Visa credit cards versus competitor rates of 3-5%
Birthday month loan discounts of 0.50% on most approved loans
Skip-a-pay option available twice yearly for only $30 per loan
Recently reduced home equity rates (5.49%-5.99% depending on term)
Online loan applications with document upload and streamlined processing
Full-service credit union with competitive certificate rates (3.50% APR on 9-month special)
NCUA-insured member accounts with member-owned governance structure
cons: Limited staff (only 3 employees) may result in slower loan processing and customer service response times
Single physical office location in Dunbar, PA limits in-person accessibility for non-local members
Membership eligibility restricted to Fayette County residents, workers, or those who worship there
No mention of mobile banking app or 24/7 automated support services
Smaller product range and lower account limits compared to regional or national credit unions
best_for: Fayette County, PA residents and employees seeking personalized banking with lower fees
Individuals who value member-owned credit unions over for-profit banks
Borrowers seeking competitive home equity and auto loan rates with birthday month discounts
Members seeking zero-fee credit card transfers and skip-a-pay loan flexibility
diagnosis: ACE FCU is ideal for Fayette County residents who prioritize personal service, competitive rates, and credit union member-ownership over convenience and modern digital features. The main caveat is that their small staff size (3 employees) and single-location model mean slower processing compared to larger institutions, making them better suited for members who aren't in a rush and value relationship banking.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Armstrong Co. Federal Emp. — NCUA-insured credit union in KITTANNING, PA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
